title: 10 precautions to take when using Salix alba (White Willow) for medicinal purposes
intro: When using Salix alba, or white willow bark, for medicinal purposes, it is important to take certain precautions. First, it is essential to only use white willow bark that has been harvested and processed properly, as improperly prepared bark can contain harmful compounds. Additionally, white willow bark should not be used by individuals who are allergic to aspirin or other salicylates. It is also not recommended for use in children or teenagers, due to the risk of Reye's syndrome. Furthermore, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using white willow bark, as its safety in these populations has not been established. Finally, it is crucial to speak with a healthcare provider before using white willow bark, particularly for individuals who are taking any medications or have any underlying health conditions.
